pullRequest.title: DOM-74899 Integrate Apps into Elastic Search

nodes.body: At the moment, taxonomyTagLabels and taxonomyTagDescriptions aren't used. But, in the next PR, I plan to integrate an Apps tags such that an App can be searched for based on it's tags labels/descriptions

thought: for future us and posterity 😄 
`override def project: Project = ???` follows the same pattern as other places in the code so this is consistent with the codebase but it could be problematic since `Project` is required.
The real fix would be making project optional in the `SearchResult` trait, but that's a larger refactor and out of scope here. 
All good as-is!

Good callout — agreed this is tech debt inherited from the trait. The optional refactor makes sense as a follow-up but is out of scope here. We've been throwing around the idea for a larger ES refactor, I will add a ticket for this

question: I see we're indexing on archive as well. Will this return archived apps as well?

If there isn't a unit test for this case, maybe we could add one

Good catch! `findById` in `MongoModelProductRepository` filters out archived apps `(isArchived == false),` so getApp returns None for archived apps. However, the previous `indexAppById` would throw an `IllegalArgumentException` in that case rather than cleaning up the index entry. I've fixed this so that when getApp returns None (archived/deleted), we now call `deleteDocument` to remove it from the ES index instead.
nit: maybe this and the log line below should be a debug level
question: this loads all apps into memory at once. For deployments with a large number of apps, is this a concern?

This is a reasonable concern- at the current scale of our cloud customers, I don't see this being an issue but definitely something we should consider for our customer managed deployments. They better way would be to "stream" apps, so we'd need listApps (or a new method) to return a lazy collection, which would be a change to the AppService interface. Is it ok if I tackle this in a subsequent PR?
question: It looks like it would match everything alongside the constrained query, is it intentional? I see some of the other search methods here don't do that.

Yes, this is intentional. The `matchAllQuery` is used as a filter subquery in `makeSearchSourceBuilder` — it's the same pattern used by `searchFeatureViewsQuery` and `searchDatasetRwQuery`. The actual search constraint comes from the `makeConstrainedQuery` call which builds the must clause with the user's search phrase. The `matchAllQuery` filter just means "don't additionally restrict by project/environment/user" — apps are globally searchable (with post-query authorization filtering in `canUserViewApp`)
